title: Keep Unexpected Text Available
summary: Build and verify css text wrapping overflow in long URL translated heading and vertical-text incident through prediction, inspection, explanation, cumulative code, and changed-case transfer.
objectives: Explain css text wrapping overflow from purpose, behavior, and evidence.
Apply css text wrapping overflow without discarding earlier valid work.
Diagnose the named misconception in changed content, input, and failure states.

options.text: Long URL translated heading and vertical-text incident case 1: ellipsis is safe for any content because users understand hidden text exists.

Long URL translated heading and vertical-text incident case 1: allow natural wrapping, add language-aware hyphenation or emergency token breaks, and keep essential text fully available without fixed-height clipping.
Long URL translated heading and vertical-text incident case 1: set white-space nowrap and text-overflow ellipsis on every heading to preserve card alignment.

steps.title: 04 · Read the long URL translated heading and vertical-text incident case · theory

content.text: Text may contain long words, URLs, identifiers, translated labels, user spacing, or vertical writing. Overflow-wrap anywhere breaks otherwise unbreakable tokens; hyphens auto needs correct language metadata and dictionary support; word-break changes break opportunities. Ellipsis hides text and requires another complete access path, so essential content should usually wrap instead. Apply it to long URL translated heading and vertical-text incident.
